description.en:
## Overview

Ao Thung Makham is a sheltered bay about 12 kilometers south of Chumphon town, serving as both a working fishing community and the main ferry departure point for boats to Koh Tao and the Gulf islands. The bay's calm, protected waters are lined with stilted waterfront restaurants built directly over the water, where fishermen deliver their morning catch straight from the boats to the kitchen. For travelers heading to Koh Tao, this is where the journey begins, but the bay itself deserves a stop for its outstanding seafood and authentic coastal atmosphere.

## Seafood and Dining

The waterfront restaurants at Ao Thung Makham offer some of the freshest and most affordable seafood on Thailand's Gulf coast. Steamed sea bass with lime, garlic prawns, stir-fried crab with curry powder, and tom yum goong are local specialties, with most seafood dishes costing between 120 and 300 THB. The restaurants are simple wooden structures on stilts with views across the bay, and lunch is the best time to visit when the morning's catch has just been prepared. Several restaurants display the day's catch on ice so you can choose your fish before it is cooked.

## Ferry Connections

The Lomprayah pier at nearby Thung Makham Noi Beach is the departure point for high-speed catamaran services to Koh Tao, Koh Phangan, and Koh Samui. The catamaran to Koh Tao takes approximately 1 hour and 45 minutes. Songserm and other ferry operators also run services from nearby piers. Most travelers arrive early for morning departures, making the waterfront restaurants a convenient spot for breakfast or an early lunch before boarding.

## Practical Information

Ao Thung Makham is accessible 24 hours with no entrance fee. The seafood restaurants are typically open from 10:00 AM until 8:00 PM, with the freshest selections available at lunchtime. Getting here from Chumphon town takes about 20 minutes by car or motorbike. If you are catching an early morning ferry, arrange transport the night before as tuk-tuks can be scarce at dawn.
description.nl: Een beschutte baai ten zuiden van Chumphon stad waar vissersgemeenschappen de ochtendvangst serveren in waterkant restaurants op palen boven het water. De pier hier is het vertrekpunt voor Lomprayah catamarans naar Koh Tao. Enkele van de versste en goedkoopste zeevruchten aan de Golfkust, met gestoomde zeebaars en knoflookgarnalen als specialiteiten.

best_time_to_visit: Lunchtime for the freshest seafood; year-round destination
tips: Arrive at lunchtime when the morning catch is freshest and restaurants display the day's seafood on ice
If catching an early ferry to Koh Tao arrange transport from Chumphon town the night before
Most seafood dishes cost 120-300 THB making this one of the cheapest quality seafood spots on the Gulf coast

metaDescription.en: Visit Ao Thung Makham Bay in Chumphon. Waterfront seafood restaurants and the Koh Tao ferry departure point in a fishing village setting.
metaDescription.nl: Bezoek Ao Thung Makham Baai in Chumphon. Waterkant visrestaurants en het Koh Tao veerboot vertrekpunt in een vissersdorpsetting.
